summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of the Event Detail Page (public) and EventDetailEditor (admin). All 8 backend endpoints passed (auth, GET/PUT event details, member linking). Frontend renders all 12 sections correctly with colorful important dates, fallback committee avatars, Google Maps embed, QR code (qrserver API), sightseeing cards, gallery slider, and register CTA. Admin editor loads all sections with existing data and Save works. Mobile responsive venue grid stacks correctly on 390px width.
critical_code_review_comments: EventDetailPage.jsx (~570 lines) uses inline styles heavily; consider extracting some repeated card/section styles to className+CSS for maintainability. Not blocking.
Countdown component correctly hides itself when target date is in the past (returns null when expired). Verified — this is intended behavior, not a bug.
Both /api/public/events/{id}/details and /api/admin/events/{id}/details exclude MongoDB _id via projection {'_id': 0}. Good.
Admin PUT /api/admin/events/{event_id}/details uses upsert=True (server.py L1042-L1046) — supports both create and update flexibly.
Fallback Avatar component correctly detects both missing photo_url and load errors via onError fallback.
seed_data_creation: PUT to /api/admin/events/{eventId}/details with test data (5 colored dates, 2 committee members with fallback avatar, venue_info with map_embed_url + map_qr_link, 2 sightseeing places, 2 gallery images). Test data prefixed with TEST_.

backend.public_event_get: PASS - Event returns 200 with correct id/title/venue
backend.public_event_details_missing_returns_emp: PASS - Non-existent id returns empty dict with event_id
backend.admin_event_details_put_persistence: PASS - PUT succeeds and public GET reflects changes (dates, colors, statuses, venue_info, sightseeing, committee, gallery)
backend.admin_members_endpoint: PASS - Used by committee search to link members

frontend.hero_banner: PASS - event-hero visible with title, badges, date, venue
frontend.important_dates_colors: PASS - 5 cards with distinct bg colors (red=rgb(254,242,242), green=rgb(240,253,244), purple=rgb(250,245,255), amber=rgb(255,251,235), blue=rgb(239,246,255))
frontend.important_dates_status_badges: PASS - 'Open Now', 'Upcoming', 'Closed' badges displayed correctly per status field
frontend.committee_fallback_avatar: PASS - Both committee cards use SVG User icon fallback when photo_url is empty
frontend.hotels_section: ABSENT (no hotels configured for this event — conditional render works)
frontend.venue_qr_code: PASS - qrserver.com QR image rendered with data-testid='venue-qr-code'
frontend.countdown_timer: CORRECT - Hidden because countdown_date (2026-06-01) is in the past relative to current system date (2026-07-15). Component returns null when expired — verified via evaluate that diff is negative.
frontend.register_cta: PASS - Both hero and bottom register buttons present
frontend.mobile_responsive: PASS - Venue grid columns collapse to single column (278px) at 390px viewport
frontend.admin_editor_sections: PASS - All 11 sections present (Gallery, Countdown, About, Themes, Dates, Awards, Sponsors, Committee, Venue+Map, Sightseeing, Contacts)
frontend.admin_map_embed_field: PASS - Google Maps Embed URL input present and pre-filled
frontend.admin_qr_link_field: PASS - Map QR link input present

context_for_next_testing_agent: Event Detail Page fully functional. Backend endpoints at /api/public/events/{id}/details and /api/admin/events/{id}/details work with upsert. Frontend at /events/{eventId} renders 12 conditional sections. Admin editor at /admin/events/{eventId}/details has full CRUD for details doc. Test event ID: eb488c54-2ee9-4160-b851-548846ec1038. Countdown correctly hides when target date is in past (do not report as bug). QR code uses external qrserver.com API. Committee cards fallback to User icon SVG when photo_url is empty. Google Maps uses iframe embed.
notes: The system clock is set to July 15, 2026 (not January 2026 as the environment description states). This caused countdown_date=2026-06-01 to appear expired. Not a bug — the countdown component correctly returns null when expired.
